rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees involving ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE content, with type scopes for constants and equations, implementation scopes for assignments.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions reference only visible identifiers and avoid AADL property names as variables.
Write range assumptions and output guarantees using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Relate outputs to inputs in type-level contracts without relying on implementation-only paths.
Align helper declaration types with expression types to avoid type mismatch errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses assume/guarantee/eq/const/assign within annex agree blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees/constants; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL timing properties like Deadline or Period as AGREE variables.
